claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 15 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 15 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you enquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child. I'm enquiring about my daughter Alex - she's 15 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. So you have one daughter, Alex, who is 15 years old.

Now I need to ask you:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to understand your living arrangement with Alex:

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, she lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Based on the information you've provided:

- You have a daughter named Alex who is 15 years old
- You live in the UK
- Alex lives with you

Since Alex is under 16 and lives with you in the UK, you meet the basic eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

Let me ask one more question to complete the assessment:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for her.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 15 years old (under 16)
- You live in the UK
- Alex lives with you (meaning you're responsible for her)
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. Only one person can get Child Benefit for a child, and since you're responsible for Alex and she lives with you, you qualify.

**Please note:** You may also want to be aware of the High Income Child Benefit Tax Charge - if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay a tax charge. But this doesn't affect your eligibility to claim Child Benefit itself.

If you'd like to proceed with claiming, you can apply for Child Benefit through the official government channels.

reasonings: Alex is 15 years old, which is under 16 years old. The claimant lives in the UK and Alex lives with them, meaning the claimant is responsible for Alex. Nobody else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Under the Child Benefit rules, a person normally qualifies for Child Benefit if they're responsible for a child under 16 and they live in the UK. The claimant will usually be responsible for a child if they live with them. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one daughter, Alex, aged 15, who lives with them in the UK. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. The claimant meets all the basic eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for Alex as they are responsible for a child under 16 and live in the UK.

Nobody else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Under the Child Benefit rules, a person normally qualifies for Child Benefit if they're responsible for a child under 16 and they live in the UK. The claimant will usually be responsible for a child if they live with them. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one daughter, Alex, aged 15, who lives with them in the UK. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. The claimant meets all the basic eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for Alex as they are responsible for a child under 16 and live in the UK.
